iti42:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti42:2.1: “Monks, these two bright qualities safeguard the world.
iti42:2.4:
If these two bright qualities did not safeguard the world,
there would be no discerning of “mother,” “aunt,” “uncle’s
wife,” “teacher’s wife,” or “wife of those deserving respect.”
iti42:2.5: The world would fall into promiscuity, like rams with goats, roosters with pigs, or dogs with jackals.
iti42:2.6:
But because these two bright qualities do safeguard the world,
there is the discerning of “mother,” “aunt,” “uncle’s wife,” “teacher’s wife,” & “wife of those deserving respect.”
iti42:3.3: have strayed from the bright root,
iti42:4.1: But those in whom shame & compunction
iti42:4.3: who are mature in the holy life:
iti42:4.4: they are calm, their further becoming ended.
